text: Isomerism is a common phenomenon in organic chemistry but is not often seen in the covalent organic frameworks (COFs). Herein, we demonstrated the first example of controllable synthesis of COF topological isomers by using a unique tetrahedral OB unit precursor and diverse solvents, affording two 3D COFs with dia and qtz nets as isomers, denoted as JUC-620 and JUC-621. Their precise structures are confirmed by powder X-ray diffraction (PXRD) and transmission electron microscopy (TEM) studies. Notably, the distinct porous structures are observed in these two isomers, in which the qtz-net COF JUC-621 shows permanent mesopores up to 23 Å in diameter and a specific surface area as high as 2060 m2 g-1, which is much larger than those of the microporous dia-net COF JUC-620 (pore size around 12 Å and specific surface area of 980 m2 g-1). Furthermore, the high surface area and mesoporous structure of JUC-621 endow it with good applicability for the removal of dye solutions and iodine adsorption with a capacity of up to 6.7 g g-1 (2.3 times that of JUC-620, 2.9 g g-1), which not only demonstrates the potential applications of the mesoporous structure but also opens a new avenue to construct COF isomers and thus to increase the structural diversity of COFs and improve their applications.
explanation: The text reads like a polished scientific abstract with specific COF data (JUC-620/621, dia/qtz nets, pore sizes and surface areas). Such precise, compact phrasing can be produced by AI but is also typical of real literature, so the evidence neither confirms nor rules out AI authorship.
